title: Family history cardiac risk

red_flags.text: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.
red_flags.rationale: The TLoC section supports documenting a family history of sudden cardiac death as an assessed red flag only when transient loss of consciousness is relevant; it does not infer inherited risk or urgency in every family-history review.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4.1–1.1.4.6 — red flags and urgent cardiovascular assessment

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.
follow_up.rationale: The NICE section supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk assessment that includes family-history context, other risk factors, patient preferences, and documented discussion without calculating a score or diagnosing an inherited condition.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

source_limitations: Relative and biological relationship, exact condition, age at onset or death, sudden or unexplained death, records, ancestry, symptoms, examination, ECG, lipids, imaging, genetics, diagnosis, risk, screening, referral, and family communication remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
